citations.narrative:
The facility does not have the names of treatments, nor the dates of administration recorded in the medical records. The
program of veterinary care states that Distemper/Parvo/Hepatitis/Leptospirosis (7-way vaccine) and rabies vaccines will
be administered annually to adult dogs. While reviewing available medical records for vaccines there were 23 adult dogs
found to not have a current medical record for the administration of the 7-way and rabies vaccines. The facility
representative states the vaccines were administered in September 2025, and these dogs are up to date, but he forgot to
document this on a medical record. The last recorded 7-way and rabies vaccine was administered to the herd on October
7, 2024.
Failure to record the names and dates for all treatments administered does not allow for APHIS officials to determine if
adequate veterinary care has been provided for animals covered under the Animal Welfare Act. The licensee must ensure
that the names of all vaccines and treatments administered, and the dates of administration are recorded and maintained.
Correct from this day April 22, 2026 and going forward.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.13(b)(3) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The facility does not have the names of treatments, nor the dates of administration recorded in the medical records. The 
program of veterinary care states that Distemper/Parvo/Hepatitis/Leptospirosis (7-way vaccine) and rabies vaccines will 
be administered annually to adult dogs. While reviewing available medical records for vaccines there were 23 adult dogs 
found to not have a current medical record for the administration of the 7-way and rabies vaccines. The facility 
representative states the vaccines were administered in September 2025, and these dogs are up to date, but he forgot to 
document this on a medical record. The last recorded 7-way and rabies vaccine was administered to the herd on October 
7, 2024.  
 
Failure to record the names and dates for all treatments administered does not allow for APHIS officials to determine if 
adequate veterinary care has been provided for animals covered under the Animal Welfare Act. The licensee must ensure 
that the names of all vaccines and treatments administered, and the dates of administration are recorded and maintained.  
 
Correct from this day April 22, 2026 and going forward. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative. 
 
 n
